title: race condition: two grunks claim same task
description: two grunk loops can pick up same needs-grunk task if both poll at same time before either claims. bd update --claim should be atomic but if two loops run simultaneously both may read task as unclaimed before either writes. result: duplicate work, two branches for same task. fix: grunk should verify claim succeeded before proceeding — check assignee after claim, bail if not self.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] grunk checks assignee after bd update --claim
- [ ] if assignee != Grunk, skip task (another grunk got it)
- [ ] grunk SKILL.md updated
- [ ] no complexity demon

title: Replace 3-tier hierarchy with Grug+Grunk 2-tier system
description:
## Problem
3 agents (PO+TL+Eng) = too much. Too many tokens. Too complex. Complexity bad.

## What build
2 agents: Grug (think+review) and Grunk (plan+build).

**Grug** = was PO
- talk user, write spec in beads (caveman speak, short)
- review Grunk work for complexity+obvious mistakes
- polls: needs-grug-review, pr-ready

**Grunk** = was TL+Engineer merged
- read spec, plan, implement, commit
- caveman speak in all beads comments
- tag pr-ready when done, never self-close
- polls: needs-grunk

**Both skills work 2 ways:**
- loop mode (AGENT_LOOP_MODE set) = task-driven, exit when done
- interactive mode (no AGENT_LOOP_MODE) = conversational, stay open, no forced exit

## Token savings
- 2 agents not 3 (no TL layer)
- caveman speak in all beads (~75% shorter)
- short specs: problem + acceptance only, no elaborate breakdowns

## Label convention
- Grug creates tasks: needs-grunk
- Grunk finishes: pr-ready
- Grug reviews pr-ready: approves (close) or sends back (needs-grunk)

## Files change
- skills/product-owner/ -> skills/grug/ (new SKILL.md, caveman, dual-mode)
- skills/tech-lead/ -> DELETE
- skills/engineer/ -> skills/grunk/ (new SKILL.md, merged TL+eng duties, caveman, dual-mode)
- skills/product-owner/scripts/spawn-agents.sh -> updated names+labels
- skills/tech-lead/scripts/ -> DELETE
- skills/engineer/scripts/ -> move to skills/grunk/scripts/
- run-tl-loop.sh -> run-grug-loop.sh (polls needs-grug-review+pr-ready)
- run-eng-loop.sh -> run-grunk-loop.sh (polls needs-grunk)
- AGENTS.md -> update bd quick ref for new labels
- README.md -> rewrite for 2-tier system

## Caveman spec format
Grug writes beads like:
- description: problem in 3-5 lines caveman speak
- acceptance: bullet list, short, testable
No elaborate sections. No walls of text.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] skills/grug/SKILL.md exists, works in loop+interactive mode
- [ ] skills/grunk/SKILL.md exists, works in loop+interactive mode
- [ ] skills/tech-lead/ deleted
- [ ] skills/engineer/ deleted (or renamed to grunk)
- [ ] skills/product-owner/ deleted (or renamed to grug)
- [ ] spawn-agents.sh uses GRUG_MODEL+GRUNK_MODEL env vars
- [ ] run-grug-loop.sh polls needs-grug-review and pr-ready
- [ ] run-grunk-loop.sh polls needs-grunk
- [ ] Both skills use caveman speak in all beads interactions
- [ ] Loop mode: detect AGENT_LOOP_MODE, exit when done
- [ ] Interactive mode: no AGENT_LOOP_MODE, stay conversational
- [ ] AGENTS.md updated with new label convention
- [ ] README.md updated for 2-tier system
- [ ] bash -n passes on all .sh files
- [ ] /grug skill invokable directly in opencode (interactive)
- [ ] /grunk skill invokable directly in opencode (interactive)

- grunk = TL+engineer merged. one agent do both
- dual mode critical: loop mode exit clean, interactive mode stay open
- delete tech-lead skill entirely
- bash -n all scripts before done
- tag pr-ready when finish. grug review. no self-close.

title: Update Engineer skill to enforce pr-ready workflow in loop mode

## Problem

title: Engineer skill must set pr-ready label before closing tasks
description:
## Problem Statement
The Engineer agent is closing tasks directly after implementation without going through the TL review cycle. This bypasses quality control — the TL never reviews the code before it lands.

The correct workflow is:
1. Engineer implements work
2. Engineer creates a PR/branch
3. Engineer sets pr-ready label on the task
4. Engineer comments with PR details
5. TL loop detects pr-ready, reviews the work
6. TL closes the task (or sends back with needs-engineer)

Currently the Engineer is skipping steps 2-5 and closing directly.

## Root Cause
The Engineer skill's 'Getting Started' section (step 4+) shows bd ready and bd update --claim but the git workflow and pr-ready labeling instructions may not be prominent enough, or the Engineer is in loop mode and rushing to completion.

## Constraints
- Fix is in skills/engineer/SKILL.md — specifically the workflow and loop mode sections
- Must make the pr-ready step impossible to miss in loop mode
- Should not require a new skill, just clearer instructions

## Out of Scope
- Changing the beads label system
- Adding enforcement at the script level
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Engineer skill SKILL.md explicitly states: never close a task directly, always set pr-ready first
- [ ] The loop mode section calls out pr-ready as a required step before exit
- [ ] The git workflow section has pr-ready as a numbered step (not buried in prose)
- [ ] In the next E2E test, Engineer sets pr-ready and TL reviews before closure
close_reason: TL review complete. Spec approved and implementation task created (tech-team-379.1) with needs-engineer label for Engineer assignment.

comments.author: PO
comments.text: @TL - Bug spec ready. This is a high priority workflow fix — the Engineer is self-closing tasks without TL review, breaking the quality gate. Fix is a SKILL.md update only, no code changes. Run: bd show tech-team-379 --long

comments.author: TL
comments.text:
## TL Review Complete

**Status:** Approved ✓

**Analysis:**
The spec is well-defined and correctly identifies the workflow gap. Engineers are bypassing TL review by closing tasks directly, which breaks the quality gate.

**Root Cause Confirmed:**
- Engineer SKILL.md sections 5 (Git Workflow) and step 4+ (Getting Started) don't mention pr-ready
- No explicit prohibition against closing tasks directly
- Loop mode section is missing requirements

**Implementation Task Created:**
Created tech-team-379.1 with needs-engineer label. This task:
- Updates Git Workflow section to add Step 6: Set pr-ready label
- Adds Loop Mode Workflow section (new)
- Updates Common Mistakes to include this pattern
- Adds PR Ready Handoff section with clear instructions

**Next Steps:**
Engineer will implement the SKILL.md changes. Once complete and pr-ready, I'll review and close both this task and the implementation task.

cc @Engineer

title: Robust exit and cleanup handling for agent loop scripts
description:
## Problem Statement
The TL and Engineer loop scripts (.tech-team/run-tl-loop.sh, run-eng-loop.sh) have several exit and cleanup gaps:

1. **Orphaned opencode server processes** - If the loop is killed (Ctrl+C, SIGTERM) while an opencode session is running, the background `opencode serve` process is never killed. It leaks on the system.
2. **Loop dies on non-zero exit** - `set -euo pipefail` causes the entire loop to exit if `opencode run` returns a non-zero exit code (e.g. session error, network blip). The loop should recover and continue polling.
3. **Blind sleep for server readiness** - `sleep 3` is a fixed guess. On slow machines the server may not be ready; on fast machines it's just waste.
4. **SERVER_PID not trapped early enough** - If the script is killed between the `serve &` and `kill` lines, the server orphans with no cleanup path.

## User Stories
- As a developer running the loops, I want Ctrl+C to cleanly kill all child processes so I don't have to hunt for orphaned opencode servers
- As a developer, I want a loop crash or opencode error to be recoverable — the loop should log the error and continue polling rather than dying
- As a developer, I want the server to be confirmed ready before opencode run is called, not just after an arbitrary sleep

## Constraints
- Must work on both macOS and Linux
- Should not require additional dependencies beyond bash builtins
- Cleanup logic must be in the source files under skills/*/scripts/, not just .tech-team/ copies
- The spawn-agents.sh copy logic must also be updated to pull from the fixed source scripts

## Out of Scope
- Rewriting the loops in a different language
- Adding a process supervisor (systemd, launchd, etc.)
- Retry logic for opencode failures (log and move on is sufficient)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Ctrl+C on a running loop kills the opencode server child process cleanly (no orphans)
- [ ] SIGTERM on the loop also triggers cleanup
- [ ] If opencode run exits non-zero, the loop logs the error and continues to next poll cycle (does not exit)
- [ ] Server readiness is confirmed before opencode run is called (not just sleep 3)
- [ ] SERVER_PID is registered in a trap before the serve & backgrounding
- [ ] Fix applied to both run-tl-loop.sh and run-eng-loop.sh in skills/*/scripts/
- [ ] .tech-team/ copies are kept in sync (spawn-agents.sh copies from skills/)
close_reason: Completed: subtasks tech-team-o8x.1 and tech-team-o8x.2 both done

comments.author: PO
comments.text:
@TL - Feature spec ready for technical review and breakdown.

**Priority:** P1
**User approved:** Yes

Notes:
- The four specific issues are well-defined — TL should be able to create 1-2 focused engineer tasks
- The server-readiness check should poll the port (e.g. nc or curl) rather than sleep
- The trap should be set BEFORE the serve & line so there's no race window
- Both loop scripts are structurally identical so the same pattern applies to both
- Remember: fix source files in skills/*/scripts/, .tech-team/ copies should be regenerated from there

Full spec is in the task description and acceptance criteria fields.
Run: bd show tech-team-o8x --long

comments.author: PO
comments.text:
**Scope addition (user approved):** Also add PID file locking to prevent multiple instances of each loop running simultaneously.

If run-tl-loop.sh is already running for this project, a second invocation should exit immediately with a clear message. Same for run-eng-loop.sh. Use a lockfile in /tmp or .tech-team/ keyed to the repo path.

Additional acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Running run-tl-loop.sh twice for the same project causes the second to exit with 'TL loop already running (PID XXXX)'
- [ ] Running run-eng-loop.sh twice for the same project causes the second to exit with 'Engineer loop already running (PID XXXX)'
- [ ] Lockfile is cleaned up on exit (including SIGINT/SIGTERM)
- [ ] Lockfile is keyed per project (repo path) so multiple projects can run loops independently

comments.author: TL
comments.text:
## Technical Review (TL) - APPROVED

**Status:** Approved for implementation ✅

### Review Summary
Spec is clear, constraints are reasonable, and the feature addresses real reliability issues in agent loop scripts. The addition of PID file locking (from user comment) is a solid improvement.

### Architecture Decisions
- **Trap-based cleanup:** Standard bash pattern, cross-platform, no new dependencies ✓
- **Port polling for server readiness:** More reliable than sleep 3 ✓
- **Error recovery:** Wrap opencode run to log errors and continue polling ✓
- **PID file locking:** Prevent duplicate loop instances, keyed per-project ✓
- **Source of truth:** Maintain scripts in skills/ directories, .tech-team/ are copies ✓

### Task Breakdown (2 subtasks)
1. **tech-team-o8x.1:** Fix robust exit/cleanup and server readiness
   - Update run-tl-loop.sh and run-eng-loop.sh in skills/
   - Add trap handlers, port polling, error handling
   
2. **tech-team-o8x.2:** Add PID file locking and sync spawn-agents.sh
   - Implement lockfile logic (per-project keyed)
   - Update spawn-agents.sh to copy from skills/ sources

### Quality Gates
All scripts must pass: `bash -n <script>`

Both subtasks will be assigned to Engineer loop with needs-engineer label.

title: session: fix grug+grunk loop bugs
description:
fixes made in session 2026-04-25:

1. GUARDRAILS.md - rewrote Important section. clearer rules for skill dev perspective.

2. grug loop infinite loop - grug session was running bd ready instead of bd list --label-any pr-ready. fixed prompt to pass exact task id.

3. grunk label bug - grunk not removing needs-grunk when tagging pr-ready. fixed SKILL.md step 7 and loop prompt to use --add-label pr-ready --remove-label needs-grunk in one command.

4. grunk executing in main branch - run_agent always cd to REPO_DIR. fixed agent-loop.lib.sh to use WORK_DIR if set, fall back to REPO_DIR.

5. log polluting WORKTREE_PATH - tee in log() wrote to stdout, poisoning create_worktree return value. fixed log() to write to log file silently and echo to stderr only.

6. git worktree add stdout leak - git checkout/pull/worktree add printing to stdout. redirected all to stderr in create_worktree.

7. stale branch crash - grunk/tech-team-*-* branch left from failed run. git worktree add -b fails with fatal: branch already exists. fixed create_worktree to detect and delete stale branch before creating.

title: Add PID file locking and sync .tech-team/ copies from skills/
description:
## Technical Context
- Parent feature: tech-team-o8x
- Architecture: PID file lockfile keyed by repo path (hash of git root)
- Dependencies: tech-team-o8x.1 must be completed first (so both scripts have trap logic)

## Implementation Requirements
- [ ] Add lockfile check at start of run-tl-loop.sh (before main loop)
- [ ] Add lockfile check at start of run-eng-loop.sh (before main loop)
- [ ] Lockfile path in .tech-team/ keyed by repo (e.g., hash of git root dir)
- [ ] On duplicate: print clear message with running PID and exit with code 1
- [ ] Register lockfile cleanup in trap (EXIT, SIGTERM) for both scripts
- [ ] Update spawn-agents.sh to copy source scripts from skills/ instead of hardcoding
- [ ] All shell scripts pass bash -n syntax check
- [ ] Lockfile keyed per-project so multiple repos can run loops in parallel
design:
## Implementation Plan
1. Add lockfile function (reusable in both scripts):
   - LOCKFILE=".tech-team/.tl-loop.lock" (or .eng-loop.lock)
   - LOCK_KEY=$(cd "" && pwd | md5sum | cut -d' ' -f1)
   - Check if lockfile exists and contains valid PID
   - If running, print 'TL loop already running (PID XXXX)' and exit 1
   - Create lockfile with $$ (current PID)

2. Register cleanup in trap (must be BEFORE main loop):
   - rm -f "$LOCKFILE"
   - Trap EXIT, SIGTERM, SIGINT

3. Update spawn-agents.sh:
   - Copy run-tl-loop.sh from $SKILL_DIR/tech-lead/scripts/ to .tech-team/run-tl-loop.sh
   - Copy run-eng-loop.sh from $SKILL_DIR/engineer/scripts/ to .tech-team/run-eng-loop.sh
   - Make them executable

4. Testing:
   - Start run-tl-loop.sh in background
   - Attempt second instance, verify error message with PID
   - Kill first instance, lockfile should be removed
   - Verify second instance now starts
   - Check that spawn-agents.sh successfully copies files
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Running run-tl-loop.sh twice for same project exits with: 'TL loop already running (PID XXXX)'
- [ ] Running run-eng-loop.sh twice for same project exits with: 'Engineer loop already running (PID XXXX)'
- [ ] Lockfile cleaned up on normal exit
- [ ] Lockfile cleaned up on Ctrl+C (SIGINT)
- [ ] Lockfile cleaned up on SIGTERM
- [ ] spawn-agents.sh copies source scripts from skills/tech-lead/scripts/ and skills/engineer/scripts/
- [ ] .tech-team/ copies are now regenerated when spawn-agents.sh runs
- [ ] All acceptance criteria from parent feature are met
- [ ] bash -n passes on all scripts
- [ ] TL review approved
close_reason: Already implemented: lockfile logic present in both scripts, spawn-agents.sh copies from skills/

title: Fix robust exit/cleanup and server readiness in loop scripts
description:
## Technical Context
- Parent feature: tech-team-o8x
- Architecture: Bash trap-based cleanup with port polling for server readiness
- Dependencies: None (isolated to loop scripts)

## Implementation Requirements
- [ ] Set trap for EXIT and SIGTERM early (before serve & backgrounding) in both scripts
- [ ] Implement server readiness polling via nc or curl instead of sleep 3
- [ ] Add timeout (e.g. 30s) to server readiness check with failure message if exceeded
- [ ] Remove set -e or wrap opencode run in error handler to allow loop to recover on non-zero exit
- [ ] Log errors to stderr when opencode exits with non-zero code
- [ ] All shell scripts pass bash -n syntax check
- [ ] Trap must register before serve & line to prevent race condition
design:
## Implementation Plan
1. Fix skills/tech-lead/scripts/run-tl-loop.sh:
   - Register trap (EXIT, SIGTERM) immediately after POLL_INTERVAL/MODEL setup
   - Trap should: kill SERVER_PID if set, cleanup any stray opencode processes
   - Replace sleep 3 with server readiness polling (nc -zv 127.0.0.1 TL_PORT in loop with timeout)
   - Wrap opencode run in: if ! opencode run ... ; then log error and continue fi
   - Test: Kill loop with Ctrl+C, verify no opencode processes remain

2. Fix skills/engineer/scripts/run-eng-loop.sh (same pattern):
   - Note: This script doesn't start a serve, but still needs trap cleanup (killed processes)
   - No server readiness polling needed for this script (no serve background)
   - Just add error handler around opencode run and trap
   - Test: Kill loop with Ctrl+C, opencode return non-zero, loop continues

3. Verification:
   - Run bash -n on both scripts
   - Test Ctrl+C exit on TL loop (should clean up serve PID)
   - Test opencode returning error code (loop should log and continue)
   - Check /proc or ps for orphaned processes
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] No orphaned opencode serve processes after Ctrl+C
- [ ] No orphaned processes after kill -SIGTERM
- [ ] Loop continues polling if opencode run returns non-zero (does not exit)
- [ ] Server readiness confirmed before opencode run (port responds to nc/curl)
- [ ] Timeout on server readiness with clear error message
- [ ] All acceptance criteria from parent feature are met
- [ ] bash -n passes on run-tl-loop.sh and run-eng-loop.sh
- [ ] Changes in source files: skills/tech-lead/scripts/run-tl-loop.sh and skills/engineer/scripts/run-eng-loop.sh
- [ ] TL review approved
close_reason: Fixed: trap cleanup, server readiness polling, error handling all implemented

title: Create GUARDRAILS.md for tech-team repo
description:
## Technical Context
This task implements the feature spec from tech-team-jr6. The tech-team repo is a bash/markdown repo with no traditional build system.

## Implementation Requirements
- [ ] Create GUARDRAILS.md at repo root following the Engineer skill template
- [ ] Tech Stack section: bash scripts, markdown, beads CLI, opencode
- [ ] Quality gates: Use bash -n syntax check for all .sh files in repo
- [ ] Key Files section: List AGENTS.md, README.md, skills/*/SKILL.md, .tech-team/*.sh
- [ ] Common Patterns section: Describe skill file format (YAML frontmatter + SKILL.md structure)
- [ ] No other changes to repo structure

## Acceptance
See parent task tech-team-jr6 acceptance criteria.
design:
## Implementation Plan

1. **Create GUARDRAILS.md** at /Users/bernardvandermerwe/Work/tech-team/GUARDRAILS.md

2. **Structure:**
   - Tech Stack: bash, markdown, beads, opencode
   - Quality Gates: Single command checking all .sh files with bash -n
   - Key Commands: Reference beads commands from AGENTS.md
   - Key Files: AGENTS.md, README.md, skills/*, .tech-team/*
   - Common Patterns: Explain YAML frontmatter + SKILL.md convention
   - Gotchas: (optional) Any repo-specific notes
   - Branch Strategy: Reference existing git setup
   - Communication: beads for tracking, issue IDs like tech-team-XXXX

3. **Quality check:** Verify file is valid markdown and bash commands work

Reference template is in /Users/bernardvandermerwe/.opencode/skills/engineer/SKILL.md
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] GUARDRAILS.md file created at repo root
- [ ] Tech stack section complete and accurate
- [ ] Quality gates section has runnable bash syntax check command
- [ ] Key files section lists required files
- [ ] Common patterns section describes skill YAML + SKILL.md format
- [ ] File is valid markdown (no syntax errors)
- [ ] Engineer can run 'bd ready' on tech-team without asking setup questions
- [ ] TL review approved
close_reason: Implementation complete and approved - GUARDRAILS.md created, quality gates passing, all acceptance criteria met

title: Add GUARDRAILS.md to tech-team repo - Enable Engineer skill to work on this project
description:
## Problem Statement
The tech-team repo has no GUARDRAILS.md file. The Engineer skill requires this file to understand the project's tech stack, quality gates, and conventions. Without it, the Engineer loop cannot safely work on this repo - it will either ask the user questions (breaking automation) or make incorrect assumptions about patterns.

This also blocks the end-to-end test of the full PO → TL → Engineer pipeline on this repo.

## User Stories
- As an Engineer agent, I want a GUARDRAILS.md so I can implement tasks on tech-team without asking setup questions
- As a developer using this system, I want the pipeline to be self-demonstrating by running on its own repo

## Constraints
- This is a shell-scripts/markdown repo - no build system, no package manager, no tests in the traditional sense
- Quality gates must be appropriate for a skill/script repository (e.g. bash -n syntax check, markdown lint, or simply 'scripts are executable and valid bash')
- Must follow the GUARDRAILS.md template from the Engineer skill

## Out of Scope
- Adding a build system or test framework to tech-team
- Changing the repo structure
- Any code changes beyond creating the GUARDRAILS.md file
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] GUARDRAILS.md exists at repo root
- [ ] Tech stack section accurately describes the repo (bash scripts, markdown, beads, opencode)
- [ ] Quality gates section has at least one runnable command (e.g. bash syntax check on all .sh files)
- [ ] Key files section lists AGENTS.md, README.md, skills/*/SKILL.md, .tech-team/*.sh
- [ ] Common patterns section describes the skill file format (YAML frontmatter + SKILL.md)
- [ ] Engineer loop can start on this repo without asking setup questions
close_reason: Feature approved and implemented - GUARDRAILS.md successfully created for tech-team repo

comments.author: PO
comments.text:
@TL - Feature spec ready for technical review and breakdown.

**Priority:** P2
**User approved:** Yes

Notes:
- This is a markdown/bash repo with no build system - quality gates should be bash syntax checks on .sh files
- This is the E2E test feature: the Engineer loop will implement this on the repo itself
- Keep it small - one task for the Engineer should be sufficient
- The Engineer skill's GUARDRAILS.md template is in skills/engineer/SKILL.md

Full spec is in the task description and acceptance criteria fields.
Run: bd show tech-team-jr6 --long

**Technical Assessment:**
- Feature is feasible and well-scoped
- Acceptance criteria are clear and testable
- No blockers identified

**Task Created:** tech-team-jr6.1 - Create GUARDRAILS.md for tech-team repo

This task is ready for Engineer pickup. Quality gates focus on bash syntax validation, appropriate for a bash/markdown repo with no build system.
